rationale.R1.2: The section makes a chain of causal-mechanistic claims (CLS attention entropy → correctness correlation → temperature output → calibrated distribution → distribution-free transfer) whose internal consistency cannot be assumed correct under a generic 'help' delegation.
rationale.R1.3: Concrete empirical claims are present (35–45% ECE reduction, 12 downstream benchmarks, severity-1–5 corruption) that may or may not be supported by the cited results; alignment between these numbers and the underlying experiments is a load-bearing responsibility if the delegation includes technical review.
rationale.R1.4: The section explicitly positions ConfViT against 'prior post-hoc calibrators that operate purely on output logits'; assessing whether the claimed delta is accurately characterised relative to nearest prior work (e.g., camera-ready temperature scaling variants, SPLINE, etc.) is central if novelty scrutiny is intended.
rationale.R1.6: A plain 'help with this section' most commonly signals writing-quality work; sentence-level clarity, paragraph cohesion, and precision of phrasing are all plausible primary targets.
